medications.drug_name: in-person haemostasis (firm prolonged pressure + elevation; silver nitrate/alginate/cautery) + protective dressing
medications.indication_rationale: Control the bleeding (staff pressure was insufficient); elevate the limb; keep the dressing intact.

medications.drug_name: urgent fast-track surgical/oncology referral for excision + nodal evaluation
medications.indication_rationale: Definitive management of a high-risk suspected SCC with a regional node.
Do not stop apixaban unilaterally; the perioperative anticoagulation plan is for the surgical/cardiology team.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the hand elevated, leave the pressure dressing intact ~3 days, avoid knocking/using the hand, and have staff watch for re-bleeding (warm fluid under the bandage).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Maintains haemostasis and protects the fragile ulcer before surgery.

patient_education_points.point: This lump has grown quickly and has features that need to be taken seriously — it needs to be removed and tested urgently, and the lump in your armpit checked at the same time.
The bleeding was from the fragile lump, worse because of your blood thinner; keep the hand elevated and the dressing on, and staff should press firmly and call for help if it soaks through.
If the bandage soaks through and blood keeps dripping despite 20 minutes of firm pressure and elevation, staff should arrange transfer to the emergency department.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent fast-track surgical referral; GP within 48h to coordinate
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Excision/histology/staging arrangements, haemostasis, and perioperative anticoagulation planning.

must_include_items: Recognise a high-risk suspected SCC (with a regional node) and avoid false reassurance
Arrange urgent in-person haemostasis AND an urgent fast-track surgical/oncology referral (excision + nodal evaluation)
Manage the anticoagulated bleeding (pressure/elevation; ED if uncontrolled); do not stop apixaban unilaterally
acceptable_alternatives: Direct ED transfer if bleeding cannot be controlled by nursing measures
Dermatology vs surgical/plastics referral per pathway
errors_of_commission: Reassuring it as a benign/traumatic lesion
Advising the patient to stop apixaban unilaterally
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: MUST-NOT-MISS CANCER: failing to arrange urgent excision/staging (or falsely reassuring), or not managing the anticoagulated bleeding, is a critical failure.
